_howto: Delivery portfolio, grouped by programme rather than repository. Derived from the ESLABS101 / workelate GitHub account on 2026-07-22 (300 accessible repositories, 2019-2026). `repos` is the count of repositories in that programme and is evidence, not a marketing number. `named: false` means the client has not confirmed we may use their name, so the site renders the descriptor only. Run `node scripts/gencorpus.mjs` after editing.

projects.name: RockProsUSA
projects.headline: A dispatch platform across four apps and 13 quarry sites
projects.what: Aggregates producer running 13 sites on one system. We built the whole surface: a customer ordering app, a trucker app, a driver app, an admin console and the dispatch backend behind them, plus native React Native builds for the field.
outcomes: 11,200 operations hours returned in year one

projects.name: WorkElate
projects.sector: Our own product, AI-native operating system
projects.headline: A full office suite we build on and sell from
projects.what: Mail, calendar, tasks, forms, boards, docs, data, presentations, a hub, an auth service, an admin console and a public SDK. Our own agency delivery runs on it, which is why we can say what agent systems do at commercial stakes rather than in a demo.
outcomes: the agency's own delivery runs on it

links.label: Why we run on our own product

projects.name: CitiSense
projects.headline: AI marketing and sales platform for agencies
projects.what: Campaign operations, sales workflow and reporting carried by agents so that agency teams spend their hours on creative work instead of coordination.
outcomes: live with agencies and display teams in Mexico

projects.name: the Deployer platform
projects.headline: A product family: apps, admin, billing and journey mapping
projects.what: A suite of deployable business apps with a shared React component library, an admin console, subscription billing on Stripe and PayPal, a customer journey mapping module, a blog engine and a programmatic SEO service generating landing pages from templates.

projects.name: Edunomics
projects.headline: A scholarship marketplace and the platform under it
projects.what: Donors publish scholarships, invite and review applicants and award grants; applicants build profiles, apply and track status. Around it: an accounts service, an admin console, a Next.js web front end and the backend carrying all of them. 33 API endpoints on the marketplace alone.
outcomes: donor, applicant and admin surfaces live

projects.name: an AI annotation platform
projects.headline: Human-plus-model labelling, five years before the hype
projects.what: A labelling platform for machine-learning datasets: polygon and bounding-box tooling on canvas, with a detection model running in the browser to pre-annotate every image before a human touched it. Exports to Pascal-VOC XML and JSON. Four engineers, 117 commits, one quarter.

projects.name: field and trade operations builds
projects.headline: Storefronts, admin consoles and field apps for owner-run businesses
projects.what: A recurring shape of work: a customer-facing front end, an admin console for the owner and a mobile app for the people in the field, delivered as one programme rather than three vendors.
outcomes: delivered across retail, comfort furnishing and marketplace clients

projects.name: risk, analytics and data builds
projects.headline: The plumbing that decides whether a dashboard can be trusted
projects.what: Risk scoring apps, analytics front ends, financial ETL pipelines and reconciliation jobs. Unglamorous work that determines whether every number above it is defensible.
outcomes: ingestion, normalisation and reconciliation running in production
